claimText: The Sustainable Aquaculture in Mangrove Ecosystems (SAIME) initiative promotes sustainable shrimp trade to protect mangrove ecosystems, specifically operationalizing Integrated Mangrove Aquaculture in the Sunderbans.
evidenceExcerpt: SAIME is a multi-stakeholder partnership promoting sustainable shrimp trade to protect mangrove ecosystems in South Asia, specifically operationalizing Integrated Mangrove Aquaculture (IMA) in the Sunderbans.
examUse: Statement-based question on environmental initiatives and sustainable aquaculture.
distractorHooks: Operational primarily in the Gulf of Kutch
Focuses on seaweed cultivation instead of shrimp

claimText: Mangroves exhibit vivipary, a reproductive adaptation where seeds germinate while still attached to the maternal plant.
evidenceExcerpt: Mangroves feature unique root systems like pneumatophores and stilt roots, and exhibit vivipary where seeds germinate on maternal plants.
examUse: Concept-based question on plant adaptations in saline environments.
distractorHooks: Seeds remain dormant in the saline soil for years before germinating
Vivipary is an adaptation exclusive to freshwater aquatic plants

claimText: Mangroves act as highly efficient carbon sinks, storing 7.5 to 10 times more carbon per acre than tropical forests.
evidenceExcerpt: Mangroves act as Nature's Carbon Vault, storing 7.5 to 10 times more carbon per acre than tropical forests.
examUse: Statement-based question on blue carbon and climate change mitigation.
distractorHooks: Store significantly less carbon per acre than tropical rainforests
Carbon storage is limited entirely to their above-ground canopy

claimText: India recorded an 11.4 percent net increase in its mangrove cover between 2001 and 2023.
evidenceExcerpt: India recorded an 11.4% net increase in mangrove cover between 2001 and 2023.
examUse: Trend-based statement question on India's forest and tree cover.
distractorHooks: India recorded a net decrease in mangrove cover due to coastal development
Mangrove cover has remained stagnant over the last two decades

claimText: West Bengal holds the largest share of India's mangrove cover, followed by Gujarat and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
evidenceExcerpt: West Bengal holds the largest share of India's mangroves (42.45%), followed by Gujarat (23.32%) and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands (12.39%).
examUse: Correct-pair or chronological arrangement question on state-wise mangrove distribution.
distractorHooks: Gujarat has the highest mangrove cover in India
Andhra Pradesh holds the second largest share of mangroves
